title: salix caprea var. ishidoyana
intro: Salix caprea var. ishidoyana, also known as Kawa or Japanese Willow, is a variety of the goat willow tree that is native to Japan. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with slender branches and leaves that contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The medicinal properties of Kawa include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain, making it useful in treating conditions such as rheumatism and menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Kawa include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
